messages.content:
You will be given the current section title and the current page_text.
    Your job is to check if the current section starts in the beginning of the given page_text.
    If there are other contents before the current section title,
    then the current section does not start in the beginning of the given page_text.
    If the current section title is the first content in the given page_text,
    then the current section starts in the beginning of the given page_text.

    Note: do fuzzy matching, ignore any space inconsistency in the page_text.

    The given section title is 致谢& 报告说明 SPECIAL THANKS & REPORT DESCRIPTION.
    The given page_text is 致谢&
报告说明
SPECIAL THANKS & REPORT DESCRIPTION
.

    reply format:
    {
        "start_begin": "yes or no" (yes if the section starts in the beginning of the page_text, no otherwise)
    }
    Directly return the final JSON structure. Do not output anything else.

Do not include any thinking field, explanation, or reasoning. Return only the final requested JSON or text.

message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to check if the section title "致谢& 报告说明 SPECIAL THANKS & REPORT DESCRIPTION" starts at the beginning of the given `page_text`.
The `page_text` is:
"致谢&
报告说明
SPECIAL THANKS & REPORT DESCRIPTION
."

The section title is "致谢& 报告说明 SPECIAL THANKS & REPORT DESCRIPTION".
The `page_text` starts exactly with "致谢&\n报告说明\nSPECIAL THANKS & REPORT DESCRIPTION\n."
Ignoring spaces and newlines, the `page_text` begins with the section title.
Therefore, `start_begin` should be "yes".

Check format:
{
    "start_begin": "yes"
}
Return only JSON.
Done. 
Wait, let's verify carefully.
Section title: "致谢& 报告说明 SPECIAL THANKS & REPORT DESCRIPTION"
Page text: "致谢&\n报告说明\nSPECIAL THANKS & REPORT DESCRIPTION\n."
Matches exactly at the start.
Result: yes.
Output matches requested format.
Proceed. 
No extra text.
Done. 
Output:
{
    "start_begin": "yes"
}
